title: Singular and plural nouns
description: Distinguish singular and plural nouns from one another!

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the nouns in this sentence are plural?**

>Arif enchanted the Queen’s blade with many powerful spells.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
A noun is **singular** when it refers to one thing. 

A noun is **plural** when it refers to *more* than one thing. Plural nouns *usually* end in 's'.
**"Spells"** is a plural noun. Notice how the sentence says there are "many" spells.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the nouns in this sentence are plural?**

>Reuben put on goggles to protect his eyes.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
A noun is **singular** when it refers to one thing. 

A noun is **plural** when it refers to *more* than one thing. Plural nouns *usually* end in 's'.
**"Goggles"** and **"eyes"** are both plural nouns.
